title: Race-Archetype Priors Instead of Raw Race IDs
summary: Public strategy guides show large circuit-to-circuit differences in pit-lane loss and preferred stop count: COTA’s relatively low pit loss makes extra stops cheaper, while Sochi’s higher loss favors one-stops, and Monaco strategy is dominated by undercut/traffic balance.
rationale: Public strategy guides show large circuit-to-circuit differences in pit-lane loss and preferred stop count: COTA’s relatively low pit loss makes extra stops cheaper, while Sochi’s higher loss favors one-stops, and Monaco strategy is dominated by undercut/traffic balance. That structure is richer than a plain `Race` category and should transfer better across years.
implementation_hint: Create 2-4 race archetype features such as `pit_loss_band`, `degradation_band`, `overtake_difficulty`, and `wet_sensitivity`, using public guides plus train-only summaries. Interact them with `Compound`, `TyreLife_ExpectedLife_Ratio`, `Stint`, and `Estimated_Laps_Remaining`. If you build empirical race priors, compute them strictly out-of-fold by `Year+Race`.
expected_effect: Better generalization than raw race frequency/one-hot features, especially when the same venue reappears under different seasonal conditions.
risk: Manual archetype labels can be noisy, and in-fold race priors would leak race-specific stop timing.
